title: Inga biglobosa
intro: Inga biglobosa, also known as the African mango tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, malaria, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Inga biglobosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare various herbal products, including tea, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as ointments and salves.
taxonomy: Inga biglobosa is a species of plant in the family Leguminosae. It is commonly known as a tropical tree native to the African continent. Further classification places Inga biglobosa within the genus Inga. The genus Inga is part of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. This classification system is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.
uses: Inga biglobosa, commonly known as the African walnut, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
